objective: Add deterministic moderation gates for input, pre-tool, pre-TTS and outbound surfaces, require typed tool proposals, block model-generated executable code and hidden reasoning from sensitive outputs, honor emergency stop and mute state, and fail closed when moderation or authorization cannot complete.
non_goals: Do not claim comprehensive semantic moderation or replace a future configurable moderation model.
Do not execute shell, PowerShell, JavaScript, Python or other model-generated code.
Do not persist raw moderated text, hidden reasoning or detected secret values in audit/error logs.
Do not implement the M03 LLM provider or conversation loop in this slice.
contracts: Every moderation request is assigned exactly one surface: input, pre_tool, pre_tts or outbound.
Pre-tool requests require a typed capability proposal and never accept executable source or command fields.
Unknown, malformed, expired, revoked or unauthorized capability proposals are blocked through the existing policy authority.
Hidden reasoning markers and secret-bearing content are blocked before TTS and outbound delivery.
Emergency stop blocks every moderation surface and mute blocks pre-TTS and outbound surfaces.
An internal moderation, sanitation, authorization or audit failure returns a block decision instead of allowing output.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library, browser platform APIs and existing Hina safety/runtime modules.
acceptance_criteria: A table-driven critical corpus blocks generated shell, PowerShell, JavaScript and Python execution proposals.
Vietnamese and English hidden-reasoning markers cannot pass pre-TTS or outbound moderation.
Safe input and a known typed low-risk tool proposal can pass when policy permits them.
Injected policy adapter failures, emergency stop and mute return stable fail-closed reason codes.
The Dev Console sends real moderation requests to the backend and displays its decision.
Focused safety and runtime tests pass before M02 promotion.
